parameters.content:
## LinkedIn Post Generator — Fireflies + GPT-4o-mini + Google Drive + Slack

For founders, consultants, and sales leaders who want to turn every client call or meeting into a LinkedIn post automatically. When Fireflies finishes transcribing a meeting, it fires a webhook to this workflow. The transcript is fetched from the Fireflies API, processed for key insights, and passed to GPT-4o-mini which writes a scroll-stopping LinkedIn post with a hook, key learnings with emojis, a closing question, and hashtags. The post is saved as a Google Doc and previewed in Slack for review before publishing.

## How it works
- **1. Webhook — Fireflies Transcript Done** receives the meetingId from Fireflies when transcription completes
- **2. Code — Extract Meeting ID** safely extracts the meetingId from all possible Fireflies payload structures
- **3. IF — Valid Meeting ID?** validates the meetingId — invalid webhooks exit cleanly
- **5. Set — Config Values** stores your Fireflies API key, Drive folder ID, Slack channel, author name, title, and company
- **6. HTTP — Fetch Transcript** calls the Fireflies GraphQL API to get the full transcript with sentences, summaries, and sentiment
- **7. Code — Process Transcript Data** extracts conversation text, pricing and task sentences, keywords, and meeting metadata
- **8. IF — Transcript Ready?** checks if transcript data is available — not-ready transcripts exit cleanly
- **10. AI Agent — Write LinkedIn Post** uses GPT-4o-mini to write a 180–280 word LinkedIn post from real meeting insights
- **12. Code — Build Doc and Slack Message** assembles the full Google Doc content and Slack preview
- **13. Google Drive — Save LinkedIn Post** saves the post as a document to your Drive folder
- **14. Slack — Send Post Preview** posts a 350-character preview to your Slack channel for review

## Set up steps
1. In **5. Set — Config Values** — replace YOUR_FIREFLIES_API_KEY, YOUR_GOOGLE_DRIVE_FOLDER_ID, Slack channel, author name, author title, and company name
2. Activate the workflow and copy the webhook URL from node 1
3. In Fireflies — go to Settings, Developer Settings, Webhooks, and paste the URL
4. In **11. OpenAI — GPT-4o-mini Model** — connect your OpenAI credential
5. In **13. Google Drive — Save LinkedIn Post** — connect your Google Drive OAuth2 credential
6. In **14. Slack — Send Post Preview** — connect your Slack OAuth2 credential and invite the bot to your channel

## Webhook Receipt and Validation
Fireflies POSTs a meetingId here when transcription completes. A Code node safely extracts the meetingId from any payload structure. The IF node validates it — invalid webhooks exit cleanly via the Set node below.

nodes.name: Section — Config, Transcript Fetch, and Processing

parameters.content:
## Config, Transcript Fetch, and Processing
Config stores all credentials and author details. Fireflies GraphQL API fetches the complete transcript. A Code node extracts sentences, ai_filter tags, keywords, sentiment, and metadata. A second IF gates on transcript availability.

## AI LinkedIn Post Writing
GPT-4o-mini (temperature 0.8) writes a 180–280 word LinkedIn post using real transcript content. Structure: scroll-stopping hook, specific paragraph, 3–5 emoji learnings, closing question or CTA, hashtags, author sign-off.

## Doc Assembly and Delivery
Builds a complete Google Doc with the post, meeting reference, keywords, and Fireflies link. Saved to Drive and previewed in Slack simultaneously for team review before publishing.

parameters.text:
=You are a LinkedIn ghostwriter for {{ $json.authorName }}, {{ $json.authorTitle }} at {{ $json.companyName }}.

Write a LinkedIn post based on insights from a real business meeting or client call.

MEETING CONTEXT:
Meeting Title: {{ $json.meetingTitle }}
Date: {{ $json.meetingDate }}
Duration: {{ $json.durationMin }} minutes
Participants: {{ $json.participants }}
Call Sentiment: {{ $json.positivePct }}% positive

FIREFLIES SUMMARY:
Overview: {{ $json.overview }}
Gist: {{ $json.gist }}

KEY POINTS:
{{ $json.bulletPoints }}

KEYWORDS DISCUSSED: {{ $json.keywords }}

ACTION ITEMS FROM CALL: {{ $json.actionItems }}

QUESTIONS RAISED IN CALL: {{ $json.questionLines }}

TRANSCRIPT EXCERPT:
{{ $json.transcriptExcerpt }}

WRITE THE LINKEDIN POST NOW:

Follow this exact structure:

LINE 1: Strong hook. Surprising insight, bold statement, or counterintuitive lesson from this meeting. Must stop the scroll. Do NOT start with the word I or We. Maximum 12 words.

LINE 2: Blank line.

PARAGRAPH: 2 to 3 sentences expanding on the hook. Use first person as {{ $json.authorName }}. Be specific — reference real things from this meeting. Not generic advice.

LINE BREAK.

3 TO 5 KEY LEARNINGS: Each on its own line. Start each with a relevant emoji. Pull from real transcript content. Be specific, not generic.

LINE BREAK.

CLOSING: One sentence — either a thought-provoking question for the audience OR a call to action. Keep it conversational.

LINE BREAK.

HASHTAGS: 4 to 5 relevant hashtags on one line.

LINE BREAK.

SIGN OFF: — {{ $json.authorName }}, {{ $json.authorTitle }}

RULES:
- Total length 180 to 280 words
- Plain text only — no asterisks, no markdown
- Tone: human, professional, conversational — not corporate
- Do NOT mention the meeting was recorded or transcribed
- Do NOT fabricate any facts not present in the transcript
- Write as if this is a genuine personal reflection

nodes.name: 12. Code — Build Doc and Slack Message

main.node: 12. Code — Build Doc and Slack Message
